improvements.proposed_prompt_text: When writing tests that mutate sys.modules, always preserve and restore previous module objects in finally blocks to prevent inter-test coupling.
For import-contract tests, force a fresh import by removing the target module from sys.modules before import, then restore the original module cache entry afterwards.
Prefer project-level pytest import configuration (for example pytest.ini pythonpath) over runtime sys.path mutation in conftest.py unless absolutely required.
After implementing acceptance tests, run a quick pass for maintainability issues (module cache side effects, deterministic setup, and minimal global mutation) before requesting code review.
In unit tests for modules with import-time globals, avoid top-level imports where practical; import via fixture or helper so monkeypatch/environment setup is guaranteed first.
